documentation.file_docstring: pi-coding-agent SessionManager persistence quirk: - If the file exists but has no assistant message, SessionManager marks itself `flushed=true` and will never persist the initial user message. - If the file doesn't exist yet, SessionManager builds a new session in memory and flushes header+user+assistant once the first assistant arrives (good). This normalizes the file/session state so the first user prompt is persisted before the first assistant entry, even for pre-created session files.
inline_comments: Reset file so the first assistant flush includes header+user+assistant in order.
